evals.prompt: Copy the `authenticate` function from src/auth.py lines 15-30 to a clipboard called 'auth-func', then paste it into src/new_auth.py at the top of the file.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses code-clip copy with --file and --lines, then code-clip paste --json to get structured content, then writes content into the destination file, then cleans up.
expectations: Uses code-clip copy with meaningful name
Specifies --file and --lines flags
Uses --json flag on paste for structured output
Extracts .content from JSON and inserts it at correct location
Deletes or clears the clipboard entry after use

evals.prompt: Move all the helper functions from utils.py (lines 1-50) into a new file called helpers.py.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses code-clip cut to save the functions and record removal intent, removes the original lines from utils.py, creates helpers.py with the pasted content.
expectations: Uses cut (not copy) since this is a move operation
Reads removal_descriptor from cut output to identify lines to delete
Removes original lines from utils.py using Edit
Creates helpers.py with pasted content
Uses --json on cut command to get structured output

evals.prompt: I'm refactoring the payment module. Before I start, save the current state so I can compare what changed when I'm done.
evals.expected_output: Agent copies the entire src/payment.py as 'payment-before', makes changes, copies the updated state as 'payment-after', diffs them, presents results.
expectations: Creates a 'before' snapshot with code-clip copy --tag checkpoint
After changes, creates an 'after' snapshot
Uses code-clip diff to compare the two entries
Presents the diff output to the user
Clears checkpoint entries afterward

evals.prompt: Show me all the code snippets I currently have saved.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs code-clip list --json and presents the results in a readable format.
expectations: Uses code-clip list --json
Presents the entries list in a readable format
Handles empty clipboard gracefully with a clear message

evals.prompt: I need to collect the validation logic from three different files — validator.py lines 10-25, auth.py lines 40-55, and payment.py lines 5-15 — and combine them into a new file called combined_validators.py.
evals.expected_output: Agent copies each fragment with a meaningful name and tag, merges them in order with code-clip merge, pastes into the new file, and cleans up.
expectations: Copies each fragment with --tag for grouping
Uses code-clip merge to combine them in the right order
Uses appropriate separator between fragments
Creates combined_validators.py with merged content
Clears all source entries after merge
